message.content:
Download and setup rootAVD tool.
  
Tasks:
1. Clone or download rootAVD from GitHub: https://github.com/newbit1/rootAVD
   - Use git clone or curl to download the main script
   - Save to working directory: /home/cwtrocks/android-avd/
   
2. Make the script executable: chmod +x rootAVD.sh
   
3. Check what Magisk APKs are included or need to be downloaded
   
4. Read the README to understand:
   - Supported API levels
   - Usage instructions
   - Any notes about API 36/Android 15
   
5. Web search for recent updates: "rootAVD Android 15 API 36 2026"

Important: Work in /home/cwtrocks/android-avd/ to avoid /tmp space issues.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1 # This Repo will be archived at the 24th of Oct 2023 2 # Due to the forced 2FA Mumbo Jumbo from GitHub, 3 # this Repo has moved to GitLab 4 # [rootAVD](https://gitlab.com/newbit/rootAVD) 5 ### [newbit @ xda-developers](https://forum.xda-developers.com/m/newbit.1350876) 6 A Script to... 7 * root your Android Studio Virtual Device (AVD), with Magisk (Stable, Canary or Alpha) 8 * patch its fstab 9 * download and install the USB HOST Permissions Module for Magisk 10 * install custom build Kernel and its Modules 11 * download and install AOSP prebuilt Kernel and its Modules 12 13 ...within seconds. 14 15 ## Install Magisk 16 ### Download rootAVD via 17 * [Click](https://github.com/newbit1/rootAVD/archive/refs/heads/master.zip) 18 * `git clone https://github.com/newbit1/rootAVD.git` 19 20 ### Preconditions 21 * the AVD is running 22 * a working Internet connection for the Menu 23 * a command prompt / terminal is opened 24 * `adb shell` will connect to the running AVD 25 ### Use Case Examples 26 #### on MacOS 27 28 29 #### BlueStacks 4 on MacOS 30 31 32 #### on Windows 33 34 35 #### on Linux 36 37 38 #### Fake Boot.img on MacOS 39 40 41 ### How to Install ADB (Android SDK Platform-Tools) 42 * Open Android Studio -> SDK Manager -> Android SDK -> SDK Tools -> Check on **Android SDK Platform-Tools** -> Apply 43 44 45 ## rootAVD Help Menu 46 ``` 47 rootAVD A Script to root AVD by NewBit XDA 48 49 Usage: rootAVD [DIR/ramdisk.img] [OPTIONS] | [EXTRA ARGUMENTS] 50 or: rootAVD [ARGUMENTS] 51 52 Arguments: 53 ListAllAVDs Lists Command Examples for ALL installed AVDs 54 55 InstallApps Just install all APKs placed in the Apps folder 56 57 Main operation mode: 58 DIR a path to an AVD system-image 59 - must always be the 1st Argument after rootAVD 60 61 ADB Path | Ramdisk DIR| ANDROID_HOME: 62 [M]ac/Darwin: export PATH=~/Library/Android/sdk/platform-tools:$PATH 63 export PATH=$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$PATH 64 system-images/android-$API/google_apis_playstore/x86_64/ 65 66 [L]inux: export PATH=~/Android/Sdk/platform-tools:$PATH 67 export PATH=$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$PATH 68 system-images/android-$API/google_apis_playstore/x86_64/ 69 70 [W]indows: set PATH=%LOCALAPPDATA%\Android\Sdk\platform-tools;%PATH% 71 system-images\android-$API\google_apis_playstore\x86_64\ 72 73 ANDROID_HOME: By default, the script uses %LOCALAPPDATA%, to set its Android Home 74 directory, search for AVD system-images and ADB binarys. This behaviour 75 can be overwritten by setting the ANDROID_HOME variable. 76 e.g. set ANDROID_HOME=%USERPROFILE%\Downloads\sdk 77 78 $API: 25,29,30,31,32,33,34,UpsideDownCake,etc. 79 80 Options: 81 restore restore all existing .backup files, but doesn't delete them 82 - the AVD doesn't need to be running 83 - no other Argument after will be processed 84 85 InstallKernelModules install custom build kernel and its modules into ramdisk.img 86 - kernel (bzImage) and its modules (initramfs.img) are inside rootAVD 87 - both files will be deleted after installation 88 89 InstallPrebuiltKernelModules download and install an AOSP prebuilt kernel and its modules into ramdisk.img 90 - similar to InstallKernelModules, but the AVD needs to be online 91 92 Options are exclusive, only one at the time will be processed. 93 94 Extra Arguments: 95 DEBUG Debugging Mode, prevents rootAVD to pull back any patched file 96 97 PATCHFSTAB fstab.ranchu will get patched to automount Block Devices like /dev/block/sda1 98 - other entries can be added in the script as well 99 - a custom build Kernel might be necessary 100 101 GetUSBHPmodZ The USB HOST Permissions Module Zip will be downloaded into /sdcard/Download 102 103 FAKEBOOTIMG Creates a fake Boot.img file that can directly be patched from the Magisk APP 104 - Magisk will be launched to patch the fake Boot.img within 60s 105 - the fake Boot.img will be placed under /sdcard/Download/fakeboot.img 106 107 Extra Arguments can be combined, there is no particular order. 108 109 Notes: rootAVD will 110 - always create .backup files of ramdisk*.img and kernel-ranchu 111 - replace both when done patching 112 - show a Menu, to choose the Magisk Version (Stable || Canary || Alpha), if the AVD is online 113 - make the choosen Magisk Version to its local 114 - install all APKs placed in the Apps folder 115 - use %LOCALAPPDATA%\Android\Sdk to search for AVD system images 116 ``` 117 ### Linux & MacOS 118 ``` 119 Command Examples: 120 ./rootAVD.sh 121 ./rootAVD.sh ListAllAVDs 122 ./rootAVD.sh InstallApps 123 124 ./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-33/google_apis_playstore/x86_64/ramdisk.img 125 ./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-33/google_apis_playstore/x86_64/ramdisk.img FAKEBOOTIMG 126 ./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-33/google_apis_playstore/x86_64/ramdisk.img DEBUG PATCHFSTAB GetUSBHPmodZ 127 ./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-33/google_apis_playstore/x86_64/ramdisk.img restore 128 ./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-33/google_apis_playstore/x86_64/ramdisk.img InstallKernelModules 129 ./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-33/google_apis_playstore/x86_64/ramdisk.img InstallPrebuiltKernelModules 130 ./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-33/google_apis_playstore/x86_64/ramdisk.img InstallPrebuiltKernelModules GetUSBHPmodZ PATCHFSTAB DEBUG 131 ./rootAVD.sh system-images/android-33/google_apis_playstore/x86_64/ramdisk.img AddRCscripts 132 ``` 133 134

243 244 ### Notes 245 * 64 Bit Only Systems needs Magisk 23.x 246 * In the Menu, you can choose between the newest Magisk, Canary, Stable and Alpha, Version. 247 * With the new Option `s`, you can see and download any other Versions of Magisk 248 * Once choosen, the script will make that Version to your local one. 249 * Prebuilt Kernel and Modules will be pulled from [AOSP](https://android.googlesource.com/kernel/prebuilts) 250 * Starting Magisk from Terminal via `adb shell monkey -p com.topjohnwu.magisk -c android.intent.category.LAUNCHER 1` 251 * API 28 (Pie) is **not supported** at all -> [because](https://source.android.com/devices/bootloader/partitions/system-as-root#sar-partitioning) 252 * Magisk Versions >= 26.x can only be proper installed with the FAKEBOOTIMG argument 253 * due to the [New sepolicy.rule Implementation](https://github.com/topjohnwu/Magisk/releases/tag/v26.1) 254 * Android 14 needs Magisk Version >= 26.x to be rooted 255 256 ### ANDROID_HOME 257 * Default location can be overwritten by setting the `ANDROID_HOME` variable 258 * In both cases, the script will search in it for AVD system-images and adb binarys 259 * `ANDROID_HOME` Sets the path to the SDK installation directory -> [AOSP Variables reference](https://developer.android.com/tools/variables#envar) 260 261 ### Notes for Apk Developers 262 * [How-To SU](http://su.chainfire.eu) from [Chainfire's](https://github.com/Chainfire) [libsuperuser](https://github.com/Chainfire/libsuperuser) - Guidelines for problem-free su usage (for Android Developers) 263 * [TopJohnWu's libsu](https://github.com/topjohnwu/libsu) - An Android library providing a complete solution for apps using root permissions 264 265 ### Fake Boot.img Function 266 * During runtime, the script will launch the Magisk APK so that you can patch the fake Boot.img within 60s 267 * The script also detects if there is already a patched Boot.img present 268 * This feature lets you also update and switch between Magisk Versions 269 * Updating a patched ramdisk will delete the overlay.d folder, all mods are gone! 270 * If Magisk can't open a file manager, i.e. on Automotive AVDs, 271 * download and install the famous [X-plore file manager](https://www.lonelycatgames.com/apps/xplore) 272 * The script switches to user 0, so that you can see `/sdcard/Download/fakeboot.img` 273 274 ### 2 Ways to boot the AVD into Safe Mode 275 * 1st Way - If the AVD still boots normal: 276 * Tap and Hold the **Power Button** until the 3 Options appear 277 * Tap and Hold the **Power Off Button** until **Reboot to safe mode** appears 278 * 2nd Way - If the AVD stuck while booting (**black** screen): 279 * Tap and Hold the **Volume Down Button** 280 * The Time Window is between the **Launching Emulator Bar** is approx **half way** until the **Google Boot Screen** appears 281 * Confirmation 282 * On the Bottom Left Corner reads: **Safe mode** 283 284 ### Automotive Notes 285 * After patching the ramdisk.img and cycle power, switch to user 0 via `adb shell am switch-user 0` 286 * open the Magisk App and the **Requires Additional Setup** pops up -> reboot AVD 287 * switch again to user 0 288 * open the Magisk App -> Settings -> Multiuser Mode -> **User-Independent** -> reboot AVD 289 * Every time you want to Grant Su Permissions, switch to user 0 and then back to 10 `adb shell am switch-user 10` 290 * Alternative, you can install the Module [Magisk Single User Mod](https://github.com/newbit1/msum) 291 * and remove all user higher than 0 i.e. `adb shell pm remove-user 13` or `adb shell pm remove-user 10` 292 293 ### BlueStacks 4 Notes on MacOs 294 * Modules are working 295 * Zygisk doesn't work 296 * The Home Screen Apk closes as soon as Magisk APP is installed 297 * but you can start Magisk from Terminal via `adb shell monkey -p com.topjohnwu.magisk -c android.intent.category.LAUNCHER 1` 298 * and Hide the Magisk APP to Settings i.e. 299 * ADB Connection is very buggy, `adb kill-server` is necessary quite often 300 301 ### Links 302 * [XDA [GUIDE] Build / Mod AVD Kernel Android 10 / 11 rootAVD [Magisk] [USB passthrough Linux] [Google Play Store API]](https://forum.xda-developers.com/t/guide-build-mod-avd-kernel-android10-x86_64-29-root-magisk-usb-passthrough-linux.4212719) 303 * [Inject Android Hardware USB HOST Permissions](https://github.com/newbit1/usbhostpermissons) 304 * [XDA [SCRIPT] rootAVD - root your Android Studio Virtual Device emulator with Magisk [Android 12][Linux][Darwin/MacOS][WIN][Google Play Store APIs]](https://forum.xda-developers.com/t/script-rootavd-root-your-android-studio-virtual-device-emulator-with-magisk-android-11-linux-darwin-macos-win-google-play-store-apis.4218123) 305 * [rootCROS - A Script to root your Google Chrome OS installed on a non Chromebook Device](https://github.com/newbit1/rootCROS) 306 307 ### XDA [GUIDE] How to [Build|Mod|Update] a custom AVD Kernel and its Modules 308 * [[GUIDE][Build|Mod|Update][kernel-ranchu][goldfish][5.4][5.10][GKI][ramdisk.img][modules][rootAVD][Android 11(R) 12(S)][AVD][Google Play Store API]](https://forum.xda-developers.com/t/guide-build-mod-update-kernel-ranchu-goldfish-5-4-5-10-gki-ramdisk-img-modules-rootavd-android-11-r-12-s-avd-google-play-store-api.4220697) 309 310 ### How to root AVDs without Play Store (Google APIs) out of the box 311 ### Windows 312 * open a terminal -> win + r `cmd` 313 * add emulator to your PATH 314 * find your AVD 315 * launch your AVD with the `-writable-system` argument 316 ``` 317 set PATH=%LOCALAPPDATA%\Android\Sdk\emulator;%PATH% 318 emulator -list-avds 319 Pixel_4_API_29 320 emulator -avd Pixel_4_API_29 -writable-system 321 ``` 322 * open a 2nd terminal -> win + r `cmd` 323 * enter the following commands one by one 324 ``` 325 set PATH=%LOCALAPPDATA%\Android\Sdk\platform-tools;%PATH% 326 adb root 327 adb shell avbctl disable-verification 328 adb disable-verity 329 adb reboot 330 adb root 331 adb remount 332 adb shell 333 generic_x86_64:/ # 334 ``` 335 336 ### [Compatibility Chart](CompatibilityChart.md) 337

content.text:
I've successfully downloaded and set up the rootAVD tool. Here's what I found:

**Setup Complete:**
- ✅ Repository cloned to `/home/cwtrocks/android-avd/rootAVD`
- ✅ Script made executable (`rootAVD.sh`)
- ✅ Magisk.zip (11MB) included in the repository
- ✅ Empty `Apps` folder ready for APK installations

**Officially Supported API Levels:**
Based on the README and compatibility chart, the official rootAVD supports:
- API 24-26 (Android 7-8)
- API 29-33 (Android 10-13)
- **Note:** API 28 (Android 9 Pie) is explicitly NOT supported

**Important Notes about Android 15 / API 36:**

The official rootAVD repository was **archived on October 24, 2023** and moved to GitLab. However, the compatibility chart only documents support up to API 33 (Android 13).

For **Android 15 (API 35)** and **Android 16 (API 36)**, you'll need to use community forks:

1. **dynamicfire/rootAVD** - Fixes Magisk 30.x BusyBox detection issue, tested on macOS M4 with:
   - ✅ Android 15 (API 35) 
   - ✅ Android 16 (API 36)
   - ✅ Magisk 30.6

2. **qig123/rootAVD2026** - Windows-focused fork tested with Magisk v30.6 and Android 16 (API 36)

**Key Requirements for Modern Android:**
- Android 13+ (API 33+) requires using the `FAKEBOOTIMG` argument
- Android 14+ needs Magisk 26.x or higher
- Magisk 30.x changed BusyBox output format (requires patched fork)
